title: lavandula pedunculata subsp. maderensis
intro: Lavandula pedunculata subsp. maderensis, also known as Madeira lavender, is a subspecies of the lavender plant native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and calming effects, which make it useful for treating various health issues such as anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Madeira lavender include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and soothing skin conditions like acne and eczema. Madeira lavender can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or dried flower for use in aromatherapy, topical applications, and other herbal remedies.
